name: Yecla la Vieja Hillfort Circuit
name_original: Yacimiento Castro Vetón de Yecla la Vieja – circular desde Yecla de Yeltes
description: This easy 3.2-mile (5.1 km) circular hike from Yecla de Yeltes explores the ancient Vettonian hillfort of Yecla la Vieja in just over an hou
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Vitigudino are primarily found within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park, a region characterized by deep river gorges carved by the Duero River. The landscape features traditional dehesa ecosystems with scattered oak trees and impressive waterfalls. Natural viewpoints offer panoramic vistas of the river and rugged terrain. Best easy hiking trails around Vitigudino The most popular easy hiking route is Pozo de los Humos Trail, a 2.3 miles (3.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 4 minutes to complete. This path leads directly to the Pozo de los Humos Waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peña de la Vela Trail, an easy 4.1 miles (6.7 km) path. This route offers panoramic views of the Duero River and historical remnants within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park. Local hikers also love the Yecla la Vieja Hillfort Circuit, a 3.2 miles (5.1 km) trail leading through historical sites, often completed in about 1 hour 19 minutes. Hiking around Vitigudino is defined by deep river gorges, dehesa ecosystems, and impressive waterfalls. The network offers options for different ability levels, including many easy hiking trails. The routes in Vitigudino are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore Vitigudino's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available around Vitigudino?
A: There are over 25 easy hiking trails around Vitigudino, primarily located within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park. These routes are generally well-suited for a relaxed outdoor experience.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for easy hikes in Vitigudino?
A: Easy hikes in Vitigudino vary, but many are between 2 to 4 miles (3 to 6.5 km) long, taking approximately 1 to 2 hours to complete. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993119992/pozo-de-los-humos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de los Humos Trail&lt;/a&gt; is 2.3 miles (3.8 km) and takes about an hour, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993348086/yecla-la-vieja-hillfort-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yecla la Vieja Hillfort Circuit&lt;/a&gt; is 3.2 miles (5.1 km) and takes around 1 hour 20 minutes.

Q: Are there any easy circular walks in the Vitigudino area?
A: Yes, the region offers several easy circular routes. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23740433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Fraile y la Monja – Castro Vetón de El Castillo loop from Saldeana&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.7 miles (4.4 km) long. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17503601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Friar's Viewpoint loop from Aldeadávila Dam&lt;/a&gt;, a shorter 1.3-mile (2.1 km) path.

Q: Which easy trails are suitable for families with children?
A: Many easy trails are family-friendly due to their manageable distances and gentle terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993119992/pozo-de-los-humos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de los Humos Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, leading to a spectacular waterfall. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993348086/yecla-la-vieja-hillfort-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yecla la Vieja Hillfort Circuit&lt;/a&gt; also offers an engaging historical element that can appeal to children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on easy hikes around Vitigudino?
A: Most trails in the Arribes del Duero Natural Park are generally dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your pet, as water sources can be limited.

Q: Are there easy trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Absolutely! The most prominent is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993119992/pozo-de-los-humos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de los Humos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which leads directly to the impressive 50-meter-high &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4230399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de los Humos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23741337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Airón Waterfall – Duero Viewpoint loop from Pereña de la Ribera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of scenic views can I expect on easy hikes in Vitigudino?
A: Easy hikes often provide panoramic views of the dramatic Duero River gorges, traditional dehesa landscapes with scattered oak trees, and the rugged terrain of the Arribes del Duero Natural Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993141448/pena-de-la-vela-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Vela Trail&lt;/a&gt; is particularly known for its stunning vistas.

Q: What historical sites or attractions can be found along easy trails?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993348086/yecla-la-vieja-hillfort-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yecla la Vieja Hillfort Circuit&lt;/a&gt; takes you through historical sites, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5446366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vettonian Hillfort of Yecla la Vieja&lt;/a&gt;. Other trails might offer glimpses of traditional architecture or remnants of the region's past.

Q: What is the best season for easy hiking in Vitigudino?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for easy hiking in Vitigudino. The weather is milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Spring brings lush greenery and full waterfalls, while autumn offers pleasant temperatures and beautiful fall colors. Summer can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable if hiking during this time.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the easy trails in Vitigudino?
A: The easy trails around Vitigudino are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the breathtaking views of the Duero River gorges, the tranquility of the dehesa landscapes, and the accessibility of routes leading to impressive natural features like the Pozo de los Humos Waterfall.

Q: Are there any easy trails that offer opportunities for wildlife spotting?
A: Yes, the dehesa ecosystems and river gorges are home to diverse wildlife. Keep an eye out for various bird species, including raptors soaring above the canyons, and other local fauna. Early mornings or late afternoons are often the best times for wildlife observation on trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993141448/pena-de-la-vela-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Vela Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy trails near the Portuguese border?
A: Given Vitigudino's proximity to the Duero River, which forms part of the natural border with Portugal, some easy trails will bring you close to or even along the border. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19805210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barca d'Alva – Vega Terrón River Port loop from Barca d'Alva&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.8-mile (6.1 km) route that explores this cross-border area.
tips:
Archaeological site where the ruins of El Castro Castle are found, whose wall is the only remaining vestige and surrounds an area of approximately 5 hectares. Within the property is also the Hermitage of the Virgen del Castillo and tour the place through the various paths to see other remains and some stone engravings of animals and human figures.
It is estimated that the construction dates from the Iron Age.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Yacimiento Castro Vetón de Yecla la Vieja circular trail?
faqs.answer: This circular hike is relatively short and easy. Most hikers complete the approximately 5.1 km (3.2 miles) route in about 1 hour and 20 minutes, depending on their pace and how much time they spend exploring the archaeological site.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally considered easy and suitable for beginners. While there are sections with 'piedras hincadas' (pointed stones embedded in the ground) that might make the terrain uneven, the overall accessibility of the site as a tourist attraction suggests it's manageable for most hikers, including families.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The circular trail typically starts from Yecla de Yeltes. While specific parking details aren't provided, as a significant historical site and tourist attraction, there is usually designated parking available near the entrance or within the village of Yecla de Yeltes to access the archaeological site.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Yacimiento Castro Vetón trail?
faqs.answer: The trail circles an ancient fortified settlement on a promontory. You'll encounter impressive pre-Roman walls and various archaeological features. Be aware that some sections feature 'piedras hincadas' (pointed stones) which can make the path uneven. A separate path also leads down to the Varlaña stream, where you can find an old traditional mill. The surrounding landscape is typical of the beautiful 'Territorio Vetón'.

faqs.question: What historical and natural features can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail is rich in history and natural beauty. You'll explore the formidable defensive structures of the ancient Vettonian hillfort, including colossal perimeter walls, foundations of Alto Medieval structures, and a Roman gate. A key attraction is the abundance of petroglyphs carved into the stones, depicting horses, horsemen, and boars. You can also see 'cabezas cortadas' (sculpted human heads) and a 'verraco' (boar sculpture). Outside the main enclosure, there's a late Roman necropolis with exposed tombs. Don't miss the Vettonian Hillfort of Yecla la Vieja itself.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is generally during spring or autumn when the weather is milder and the natural landscape is at its most vibrant. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder temperatures, though the trail is likely accessible year-round.

faqs.question: Is the Yacimiento Castro Vetón de Yecla la Vieja trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this particular trail are not detailed, many outdoor trails in Spain allow dogs, often on a leash. Given its location within a natural park and archaeological site, it's advisable to keep dogs on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to preserve the site's integrity and respect other visitors.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail or visit the archaeological site?
faqs.answer: The trail itself does not typically require a permit or entrance fee for hiking. However, as the Yacimiento Castro Vetón de Yecla la Vieja is a significant archaeological site and tourist attraction, there might be specific access rules or fees for entering certain protected areas or interpretive centers within the site. It's always a good idea to check local information before your visit.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially overlaps with the Camino de Guadramiro for about 5% of its length, offering a glimpse into other local paths.

faqs.question: What are the regulations for hiking in Arribes del Duero Natural Park?
faqs.answer: As this trail is located within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park, visitors should adhere to general park regulations. These typically include staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife or plants, taking all litter with you, and respecting archaeological sites. Open fires are usually prohibited, and camping may be restricted to designated areas. Always check the official park website for the most current and specific regulations.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail itself is a natural path, so dedicated restrooms or water fountains directly along the route are unlikely. It's best to use facilities in Yecla de Yeltes before starting your hike and carry enough water for the duration of your walk, especially on warmer days.

waypoints.name: Vettonian Hillfort of Yecla la Vieja
